meta.title: WillChain – Your Digital Will on the Blockchain
meta.description: WillChain is an automated digital will on the blockchain. Your crypto assets will automatically transfer to your heir without lawyers or notaries.
meta.og_title: WillChain – Your Digital Will on the Blockchain
meta.og_description: Your crypto assets will automatically transfer to your heir. No lawyers. No bureaucracy. Forever on the blockchain.

hero.title: When you're gone, your assets go to your chosen heir
hero.subtitle: Every year, thousands of people pass away without leaving a successor for their cryptocurrency. Loved ones know the funds exist but can't access them. WillChain solves this automatically.

problem.label: The Problem
problem.title: What happens to your crypto after you're gone?
problem.subtitle: Most people don't think about it until it's too late.
problem.card1_desc: If you're the only one who knows the private key, your funds disappear forever when you pass. No court or notary can help here.
problem.card2_desc: Billions of dollars in cryptocurrency lie untouched in abandoned wallets. Their owners are gone, and the funds are locked forever – nobody can use them.
problem.card3_desc: Even knowing the funds exist, without the key or password, family can't access them. It's a legal dead end.

recycle.hint: If the wallet owner has completely disappeared (no activity beyond the set period + 60 days) and hasn't designated an heir – anyone can initiate the recycling of funds and receive a 1% reward.
recycle.status_inactive: Inactive – not yet abandoned (total timeout not expired)

how_it_works.label: 4 Steps
how_it_works.subtitle: Four simple steps – and your crypto is protected
how_it_works.title: How it works
how_it_works.step1_desc: Buy WILL tokens on an exchange or receive them from another holder. Your digital will activates as soon as you hold any amount of WILL.
how_it_works.step2_desc: Specify the wallet address of the person who should receive your funds after your prolonged inactivity or death. This could be a spouse, child, friend – anyone you care about.
how_it_works.step3_desc: Any outgoing transaction automatically confirms your activity. If the wallet hasn't been used in a while, click 'I'm here!' manually.
how_it_works.step4_desc: Set how long of inactivity triggers the WillChain mechanism: 30, 90, 180, or 365 days. This is followed by a 30-day grace period and 30 days for the heir.

trust.noncustodial_desc: No one but your heir can access your funds

faq.label: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Answers to the most common questions
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Do I need to visit the site daily?
faq.a1: No. If you regularly use your crypto wallet, the timer resets automatically. The 'I'm here!' button is only needed if your wallet is completely unused.
faq.a2: 30, 90, 180, or 365 days. You can change this anytime. Those who trade frequently choose 30 days; those with long-term investments choose 365.
faq.q3: What happens if the period expires?
faq.a3: A 30-day grace period begins – you can still confirm your presence. Then, the heir has 30 days to initiate a claim. If no heir is appointed or they don't claim in time, funds are recycled: 47% are burned forever, 47% are distributed to WILL holders, 5% go to the protocol, and 1% to the initiator.
faq.q4: Can someone change my heir without my knowledge?
faq.a4: No. Only you, with your private key, can change the heir. The contract guarantees this.
faq.q5: Can anyone see my personal data?
faq.a5: No. Only your wallet address, balance, and activity timestamps are stored on the blockchain. No names or personal information.
faq.q6: Where is the smart contract located?
faq.a6: Deployed on the Base Sepolia testnet. The source code is open and verified on Basescan.
faq.q7: Does auto-timer reset work with multi-sig wallets?
faq.a7: If you use a standard wallet (MetaMask, Trust Wallet), the timer resets with any transfer. However, if you use Smart Wallets or Multisig (e.g., Safe), where a relayer executes transactions, you must manually confirm your activity via the button on the site.
faq.q8: Can DEX or dApp approvals affect my inheritance?
faq.a8: No. Token allowances do not affect your inheritance timer. Only your direct actions – transfers, confirmActivity, or vault settings – count as presence confirmation. A third party using transferFrom() cannot reset your timer. Your inheritance will activate even with active DEX approvals.

security.allowance_text: Only your direct actions count as presence confirmation: token transfers, activity confirmations, or vault settings. DEX swaps via allowances do not reset the timer. If you trade on a DEX, remember to confirm your activity separately.

disclaimer.title: Legal Information
disclaimer.modal_subtitle: Please read before connecting your wallet
disclaimer.s1_text: The WILL token is a utility token solely for managing digital inheritance within WillChain. Holding the token does not grant rights to profits, equity, or any rights associated with securities.
disclaimer.s2_text: The token burning mechanism is a technical tool for clearing inactive participants from the network, not a promise of market value appreciation. We do not guarantee liquidity or future asset prices.
disclaimer.s3_text: The user is fully responsible for maintaining activity or performing manual confirmations before the set deadline. Loss of access to private keys or missing the deadline will result in irreversible loss of access to funds.
disclaimer.s4_text: The protocol is deployed on Base (Ethereum L2). You agree to the risks associated with smart contract vulnerabilities or blockchain network upgrades. This is experimental software – use at your own risk.
disclaimer.s5_text: The user is obliged to independently verify that the use of WillChain does not conflict with the legislation of their jurisdiction.
disclaimer.checkbox: I have read and accept the terms. I understand that I am fully responsible for my activity and potential loss of assets.

tiers.title: Vault Tier
